name: Mushroom Masala
tagline: Button mushrooms in onion-tomato gravy — vitamin D, copper, selenium
highlight: UV-exposed mushrooms are one of the only plant sources of vitamin D2

hero.alt: Sliced button mushrooms in a deep red-brown gravy with coriander on top
hero.caption: mushroom masala — onion-tomato base, deep umami

healthFacts: Sun-exposed mushrooms can hit 500IU vitamin D per cup — rare for plant foods.
Selenium and copper in mushrooms support thyroid and antioxidant systems.
Glutamate-rich — natural umami without animal protein.
Beta-glucans support immune function and may modestly lower LDL.

steps.title: Sear the mushrooms first
steps.detail: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wide pan, add the sliced mushrooms in a single layer with a pinch of salt. Cook on high for 4 minutes without stirring, then toss for another 2. They should brown and release their water.
steps.tip: Crowding = steaming. Sear in batches if your pan is small.

steps.title: Make the masala
steps.detail: In the same pan, add butter and onion, cook till golden. Add ginger-garlic, then tomato purée and the cashew paste (blend cashews with 2 tbsp water). Cook 6 minutes till oil separates. Add spices.
steps.tip: Cashew paste gives the gravy body without cream — better for daily eating.

steps.title: Combine and finish
steps.detail: Tip the seared mushrooms back in along with their released juices. Add ½ cup water, simmer 5 minutes. Stir in garam masala. Garnish with coriander.
steps.tip: Sun the mushrooms gill-side up for 30 min before cooking to multiply vitamin D.
